_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to go beyond simply stating what we think by considering different viewpoints and using evidence to support our ideas.

_source.w: We are learning how to go beyond simply stating what we think by considering different viewpoints and using evidence to support our ideas.
y: Backing up our ideas with evidence and acknowledging other viewpoints makes our arguments much stronger.
This is the foundation of critical thinking and academic writing.
Learning to consider different perspectives helps us engage more thoughtfully with complex issues.
eg: We rewrite a bare assertion like 'Dogs are the best pets' by adding evidence and acknowledging another viewpoint.
We find examples in texts where authors use authoritative sources to support their claims.
We practise using phrases like 'According to…', 'Research shows…' and 'Some people argue…'.
We identify when a text only presents one side and discuss what is missing.
We write a balanced paragraph that presents two different views on a topic.
We evaluate the strength of different arguments by examining their evidence.
